title: Impatiens atrosanguinea
intro: Impatiens atrosanguinea, also known as the dark red jewelweed, is a species of flowering plant in the balsam family (Balsamaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties and is traditionally used to treat various health issues, including skin irritations and infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Impatiens atrosanguinea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Impatiens atrosanguinea is commonly prepared into a topical cream or tincture to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Impatiens atrosanguinea is classified under the family Balsamaceae. However, some sources place it in the family Dipterocarpaceae or under a broader classification of Balsamoideae. The genus Impatiens belongs to the order Ericales. This classification may vary depending on the source and the most recent taxonomic revisions. Further research may be needed to confirm the exact classification of Impatiens atrosanguinea.
uses: Impatiens atrosanguinea, also known as the dark red balsam, is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. In some cultures, the plant is also used to treat fever and respiratory issues. The roots of Impatiens atrosanguinea are believed to have diuretic properties, which may help in treating urinary problems. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Impatiens atrosanguinea for medicinal purposes.

The plant's roots are traditionally used to make a medicinal tea that is said to help with respiratory issues and coughs. side_effects: Impatiens atrosanguinea, also known as the dark red balsam, is a plant that is native to tropical regions.
